# Cover Letter Format Iteration — Design Spec

**Date:** 2026-05-08
**Status:** Approved (5/5 sections)
**Owner:** Yash Anghan
**Driver problem:** The first cover letter generated by the new pipeline (LDS — Senior Full Stack Engineer, run earlier on 2026-05-08) does not match the user's intended formatting. The user pasted a reference `.tex` showing the desired layout (leaner preamble, recipient address block, `Re:` line, paragraph-skip-driven spacing, no portfolio link) and asked for the cover-letter prompt to emit that layout instead. Same change also requires removing the portfolio link from the resume prompt's header.

This spec is the second iteration of the cover-letter pipeline. The original spec at `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-08-cover-letter-pipeline-design.md` is left intact as a historical record.

---

## 1. Goal

Two changes:

1. **Cover-letter LaTeX template overhaul.** Replace the embedded LaTeX template inside `cover-letter-system-based-on-jd-and-resume.md` with the layout the user specified in their reference `.tex`. The new layout uses a leaner preamble, a left-aligned date, a `Hiring Manager` / company / location address block, a bold `Re: <role>` line, and global `\setlength{\parskip}{8pt}` spacing instead of explicit `\par\vspace{...}` between paragraphs. The closing changes from `Sincerely,\\ Yash Anghan` to `Sincerely,` then `Yash Anghan` on a new paragraph (`\parskip` handles the gap).

2. **Portfolio link removal from both prompts.** Remove the `\href{...faGlobe...Portfolio}` line from the header block in both:
   - The cover-letter LaTeX template (handled inline in change #1 above).
   - The V2.0 resume prompt at `resume-optimization-system-based-on-job-description.md`.

The resume-prompt edit is permitted by explicit user authorization in this turn and is strictly limited to the portfolio-link removal plus the adjacent `~` separator on the GitHub line. No other resume-prompt content is touched.

## 2. Non-goals

- Refactoring the resume LaTeX template's preamble to match the cover-letter's leaner style. Out of scope; the user explicitly chose "portfolio link removal only" for the resume.
- Regenerating already-shipped resume or cover-letter PDFs (League Inc. and LDS). Those remain historical artifacts; the user can re-run those URLs through the pipeline later if a portfolio-free version is wanted.
- Modifying the orchestrator (`yash-resume-pipeline.mjs`) or the per-URL loop in `modes/yash-resume-pipeline.md`. The compile path is template-agnostic.
- Modifying `generate-pdf-latex.mjs`, `modes/pipeline.md`, or `modes/auto-pipeline.md` (existing hard rules).
- Applying the tectonic `\ifdefined\pdfgentounicode...\fi` guard to the resume prompt. Separate concern; the user-layer note already covers it at run time.
- Adding new automated tests beyond what already exists. The visual-eyeball gate is the right level for layout verification.

## 3. Files modified

| Path | Change |
|------|--------|
| `cover-letter-system-based-on-jd-and-resume.md` | Replace `<base_latex_template>` with the new template (Section 4 below). Update Phase 2 ¶4 closing-line spec, two Phase 5 verification steps, and `<execution_command>` placeholder substitutions (Section 5 below). |
| `resume-optimization-system-based-on-job-description.md` | Surgical edit to one block of the embedded LaTeX template: drop the trailing `~` on the GitHub line and delete the entire `\href{...faGlobe...Portfolio}` line that follows. No other content touched. |

## 4. New cover-letter LaTeX template

The cover-letter prompt's `<base_latex_template>` block is replaced wholesale with the parameterized version below. Variable placeholders use the form `[INSERT_X]` so the LLM substitutes them at emit time.

```latex
\documentclass[11pt,letterpaper]{article}
\usepackage[empty]{fullpage}
\usepackage[hidelinks]{hyperref}
\usepackage[english]{babel}
\usepackage{fontawesome5}
\usepackage{xcolor}

\addtolength{\oddsidemargin}{-0.6in}
\addtolength{\evensidemargin}{-0.6in}
\addtolength{\textwidth}{1.2in}
\addtolength{\topmargin}{-0.7in}
\addtolength{\textheight}{1.4in}

\pagestyle{empty}
\raggedright
\setlength{\parindent}{0pt}
\setlength{\parskip}{8pt}

\begin{document}

%----------HEADING----------
\begin{center}
{\Huge \scshape Yash Anghan} \\ \vspace{2pt}
\small \raisebox{-0.1\height}\faEnvelope\ \href{mailto:yashanghan97@gmail.com}{yashanghan97@gmail.com} ~
\raisebox{-0.1\height}\faPhone\ +1 (437) 290-2005 ~
\href{https://www.linkedin.com/in/yash-aiautomation/}{\raisebox{-0.2\height}\faLinkedin\ \underline{Linkedin}} ~
\href{https://github.com/yash-ai-automation}{\raisebox{-0.2\height}\faGithub\ \underline{GitHub}} ~
\vspace{-8pt}
\end{center}

\vspace{-17pt}
\noindent\rule{\textwidth}{0.4pt}
\vspace{4pt}

%----------DATE & ADDRESS----------
[INSERT_DATE_LONG]

Hiring Manager \\
[INSERT_COMPANY_NAME] \\
[INSERT_COMPANY_LOCATION]

\textbf{Re: [INSERT_ROLE_TITLE]}

%----------BODY----------
Dear Hiring Manager,

[PARAGRAPH 1: Hook — 3–4 sentences. Names role + company. Leads with exit-story. One hero metric.]

[PARAGRAPH 2: Why I match — 4–5 sentences. JD keyword echo. 2–3 approved proof points. \textbf{} on at least 2 high-priority JD keywords echoing the resume.]

[PARAGRAPH 3: Why this company — 3–4 sentences. Specific JD-supplied company detail. Why it matters to candidate.]

[PARAGRAPH 4: Close — 2–3 sentences. Forward-looking action line.]

Sincerely,

Yash Anghan

\end{document}
```

### Differences from the prior template

| Aspect | Prior template | New template |
|---|---|---|
| Preamble packages | 14 (latexsym, marvosym, multicol, fancyhdr, titlesec, …) | 6 (fullpage, hyperref, babel, fontawesome5, xcolor) |
| Margins | `-0.7in / 1.4in / -0.8in / 1.6in` | `-0.6in / 1.2in / -0.7in / 1.4in` |
| Page style | `\pagestyle{fancy}` + `\fancyhf{}` | `\pagestyle{empty}` |
| Paragraph spacing | `\par\vspace{6pt}` per paragraph; `\par\vspace{12pt}` before close | Global `\setlength{\parskip}{8pt}` |
| Tectonic guard | `\ifdefined\pdfgentounicode...\fi` block | None — the new template never uses `\pdfgentounicode` |
| Header icons | Email, Phone, Linkedin, GitHub, **Portfolio** | Email, Phone, Linkedin, GitHub (Portfolio dropped) |
| Horizontal rule | None | `\noindent\rule{\textwidth}{0.4pt}` below header |
| Date placement | Right-aligned via `\hfill` | Left-aligned, `Month DD, YYYY` |
| Recipient block | None | `Hiring Manager` + company + location |
| `Re:` line | None | `\textbf{Re: <role>}` |
| Closing | `Sincerely,\\\\ Yash Anghan` (line break) | `Sincerely,` then `Yash Anghan` (paragraph break via `\parskip`) |

### Variable substitution rules

The LLM resolves four placeholders at LaTeX-emit time:

- `[INSERT_DATE_LONG]` → today's date as `Month DD, YYYY` with zero-padded day (e.g., `May 08, 2026`).
- `[INSERT_COMPANY_NAME]` → JD frontmatter `company` field verbatim.
- `[INSERT_COMPANY_LOCATION]` → JD frontmatter `location` field verbatim. **If missing/null/empty, OMIT the entire location line.** Do not leave the placeholder text or render an empty line.
- `[INSERT_ROLE_TITLE]` → JD frontmatter `role` field verbatim.

The four `[PARAGRAPH N: …]` placeholders are also substituted at emit time, with the actual prose composed in Phase 2 (4 body paragraphs total).

## 5. Cover-letter prompt phase updates

Three other parts of `cover-letter-system-based-on-jd-and-resume.md` need surgical edits beyond the LaTeX template swap.

### 5.1 Phase 2, paragraph 4 (Close) — closing-line spec

Find the `<paragraph number="4" purpose="Close" sentences="2-3">` block. Replace the line:
```
<required>Sign-off line: Sincerely,\\ Yash Anghan</required>
```
with:
```
<required>Sign-off: write `Sincerely,` then a blank-line paragraph break, then `Yash Anghan` on its own paragraph. Do NOT use `\\` line breaks — the `\setlength{\parskip}{8pt}` preamble setting handles vertical spacing.</required>
```

### 5.2 Phase 5 — verification step replacements

Two Phase 5 steps no longer match the new template.

Replace:
```
Verify exactly 4 body paragraphs, separated by `\par\vspace{6pt}`. The final paragraph (¶4) must be followed by `\par\vspace{12pt}` before the `Sincerely,` closing.
```
with:
```
Verify exactly 4 body paragraphs separated by blank lines. The global `\setlength{\parskip}{8pt}` preamble setting handles vertical spacing — do NOT insert explicit `\par\vspace{...}` between paragraphs.
```

Replace:
```
Verify closing line is "Sincerely,\\ Yash Anghan" (with the exact \\ command).
```
with:
```
Verify the closing is `Sincerely,` on its own line, followed by a blank line, followed by `Yash Anghan` on its own line. No `\\` line breaks anywhere in the body or closing.
```

### 5.3 `<execution_command>` — placeholder substitution steps

Replace step 7 (the date-substitution step) with two steps:

```
<step number="7">Substitute the four template placeholders in the LaTeX output:
  - [INSERT_DATE_LONG] → today's date as `Month DD, YYYY` with zero-padded day (e.g., `May 08, 2026`).
  - [INSERT_COMPANY_NAME] → JD frontmatter `company` field verbatim.
  - [INSERT_COMPANY_LOCATION] → JD frontmatter `location` field verbatim; if missing/null/empty, OMIT the location line entirely (do NOT leave the placeholder text or an empty line).
  - [INSERT_ROLE_TITLE] → JD frontmatter `role` field verbatim.
</step>
<step number="8">Replace each [PARAGRAPH N: …] placeholder in the LaTeX with the actual paragraph composed in Phase 2 (4 body paragraphs total).</step>
```

### 5.4 Untouched phase content

Everything else in the cover-letter prompt stays exactly as it is post-merge of PR #4:

- Phase 1 (JD analysis + resume_keyword_echo_set + archetype detection).
- Phase 2 paragraphs 1, 2, 3 specs.
- Phase 3 (locked proof points, archetype allocation, invention check).
- Phase 4 (keyword injection, LaTeX escapes, Unicode rule).
- Phase 5 other steps (sentence count 12–16, proof-point traceability, keyword count 4–7, escape correctness, salutation `Dear Hiring Manager,`).
- Phase 6 (scoring rubric ≥90/100 and output rules priority hierarchy).
- Hard-fail tokens (`PARAGRAPH_COUNT_ERROR`, `PROOF_POINT_VIOLATION`).

## 6. Resume prompt portfolio-link removal

`resume-optimization-system-based-on-job-description.md` was previously locked behind a hard rule. The user explicitly authorized this single edit in their turn.

### Before edit (around lines 994-997 of the V2.0 prompt's embedded LaTeX template):

```latex
\href{https://www.linkedin.com/in/yash-aiautomation/}{\raisebox{-0.2\height}\faLinkedin\ \underline{Linkedin}} ~
\href{https://github.com/yash-ai-automation}{\raisebox{-0.2\height}\faGithub\ \underline{GitHub}} ~
\href{https://yash-anghan-ai-automatio-15hmplk.gamma.site/}{\raisebox{-0.2\height}\faGlobe\ \underline{Portfolio}}
\vspace{-8pt}
```

### After edit:

```latex
\href{https://www.linkedin.com/in/yash-aiautomation/}{\raisebox{-0.2\height}\faLinkedin\ \underline{Linkedin}} ~
\href{https://github.com/yash-ai-automation}{\raisebox{-0.2\height}\faGithub\ \underline{GitHub}}
\vspace{-8pt}
```

Two changes on adjacent lines: (1) drop the trailing `~` after the GitHub line, (2) delete the entire `Portfolio` line. Nothing else in the resume prompt is touched — no phase changes, no scoring change, no baseline-sentence change.

## 7. Branch & PR strategy

All commits land on the existing `feat/cover-letter-pipeline` branch. PR #4 (`feat: cover-letter pipeline`) picks them up automatically. After the changes are pushed, append a short "## Format iteration" section to the PR description summarizing what changed and why.

No new branch. No new PR.

## 8. Testing strategy

### Automated (regression gate)

- All 52 existing unit tests must still pass. The prompt edits don't touch any code path the suite exercises.
- No new automated tests added. The page-count check considered in brainstorming was skipped — the tectonic-output parser would be brittle, and the visual eyeball is the better gate.

### LLM + visual (manual smoke test)

After the prompt edits:

1. Pull the next pending URL from `data/pipeline.md`.
2. Run the per-URL loop end-to-end (JD fetch → resume + cover letter PDFs → mark processed → JSONL log).
3. Eyeball both PDFs:
   - **Resume:** header has Email + Phone + Linkedin + GitHub only. No Portfolio icon, no broken trailing `~`.
   - **Cover letter:** matches the reference layout — leaner header without Portfolio, horizontal rule below the header, left-aligned date in `Month DD, YYYY` format, `Hiring Manager` / company / location address block, bold `Re: <role>` line, 4 body paragraphs separated by `\parskip`-driven spacing, `Sincerely,` then `Yash Anghan` paragraph break.
4. Confirm the orchestrator-side artifacts are unchanged: sidecar logs in the right directories, pipeline.md `CL ✅` marker, JSONL `cover_letter_*` fields all present.

### Negative path

No new negative tests required. The cover-letter prompt's hard-fails (`PARAGRAPH_COUNT_ERROR`, `PROOF_POINT_VIOLATION`) and Phase 5 verification still apply. The existing `compile-cover-letter` failure-path unit tests are template-agnostic and continue to hold.

### Backward-compat sanity

- Cover-letter sidecar log format is unchanged (`score`, `deficiencies`, `status`, `resume_keywords_echoed`).
- `mark-processed` and `log` JSONL field shapes are unchanged.
- The `compile-cover-letter` subcommand's CLI signature is unchanged.

PR #4 review feedback (if any) is not invalidated by this iteration.

## 9. Risks & mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| New cover-letter template breaks the LLM's verification logic in Phase 5 | Phase 5 steps explicitly updated in §5.2 to match the new spacing model. |
| User-typed `\\` line breaks slip into the body or closing | Phase 5 verification step explicitly forbids `\\` anywhere in body/closing. |
| Missing `location` field on JD causes a placeholder string or blank line | §4 substitution rule mandates omitting the entire location line on missing/null/empty input. |
| Date format drift (YYYY-MM-DD vs Month DD, YYYY) | §4 mandates `Month DD, YYYY` with zero-padded day. The `<execution_command>` step 7 includes an example: `May 08, 2026`. |
| Portfolio removal breaks the trailing `~` separator on the resume header | §6 explicitly removes BOTH the `~` AND the Portfolio line — single atomic edit. |
| Already-shipped LDS / League Inc. PDFs still carry the old layout | §2 documents these as historical artifacts; user can re-run if regeneration is desired. Out of scope for this iteration. |

## 10. Out of scope

- Resume LaTeX preamble refactor.
- Page-count regression test for `compile-cover-letter`.
- Tectonic guard application to the resume prompt.
- PDF regeneration of already-shipped artifacts.
- Multilingual cover letters (de/fr/ja).
